title: Seed Registry, Origin Scope, Budgets, and Stop Policy · Transfer Build
summary: An independent municipal service owner supplies a different origin, markup family, dynamic boundary, accessibility need, resource budget, and injected failure and transfers seed registry, origin scope, budgets, and stop policy into a changed deny-by-default crawl-scope policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of deny-by-default crawl-scope policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Bind each seed to owner authorization, purpose, expected content class, policy version, contact, and expiry.
Define allowed schemes, hosts, ports, subdomains, redirects, and cross-origin resources with deny-by-default admission.
Specify methods, path prefixes, query keys and values, fragments, pagination, downloads, forms, and content types as testable rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Seed Registry
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 7603 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit authorized seeds through origin, path, query, multiresource budget, stop, and contact policy; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind each seed to owner authorization, purpose, expected content class, policy version, contact, and expiry.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Seed Registry.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bind each seed to owner authorization, purpose, expected content class, policy version, contact, and expiry. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Seed Registry, keep Seed Registry connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Stop Contact
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-stop-contact-1e1vpd" that uses case 3428 to admit authorized seeds through origin, path, query, multiresource budget, stop, and contact policy. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
